title: Is Software Losing Its Head?
summary: Seema Amble, Steven Sinofsky, and Elena Burger unpack one of the biggest questions facing enterprise software: what happens when AI agents become the primary users of software instead of humans? The conversation explores the rise of "headless" software, why APIs and agentic workflows are reshaping enterprise applications, and whether traditional SaaS products are becoming systems of record rather than systems of engagement. They discuss Salesforce's Headless 360 announcement, MCP, enterprise software architecture, and why AI may fundamentally change how businesses interact with their data. ...
score_reason: Exploring the shift to AI agents as primary software users offers a high-value insight and tactical perspective on future enterprise software design.
partner_takeaway: Readers learn how AI agents as primary software users are reshaping enterprise applications, signaling a shift toward headless software and agentic workflows.
partner_summary: This podcast episode discusses the rise of AI agents as primary users of enterprise software, exploring the implications for software design and workflows. It matters because this shift challenges traditional SaaS models and opens new opportunities for automation and integration. The audience should watch for how APIs and agentic workflows evolve and consider adapting their software strategies accordingly.

title: Don’t Follow Your Passion | Ben Horowitz’s Advice for New Graduates
summary: In this commencement address to Columbia University's Fu Foundation School of Engineering and Applied Science Class of 2015, Ben Horowitz challenges some of the most common advice given to graduates. Rather than urging students to “follow their passion,” Horowitz argues that people should focus on developing their strengths and making meaningful contributions to the world. Drawing on stories from his own time at Columbia, the founding of technology companies, and investments in startups like Airbnb, he explores the importance of independent thinking, conviction, and pursuing ideas that othe...
score_reason: Ben Horowitz’s advice challenges common career advice with a strong practitioner perspective and actionable mindset shift.
partner_takeaway: Readers learn to focus on developing strengths and making meaningful contributions rather than blindly following passion, offering a practical career mindset shift.
partner_summary: Ben Horowitz delivers a commencement address advising graduates to prioritize strengths and impact over following passion. This matters because it challenges a common but often unhelpful career narrative. The audience should reflect on their own career strategies and consider focusing on skill development and value creation.
